page: Amazon Web Services r8a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 16 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core performance. The instance achieves a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 3782.19 and a Geekbench single-core score of 2822.0, both placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Single-threaded data processing is highly efficient, with gzip compression reaching 41.51 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 18.32 GB/sec. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks, such as a multi-core Geekbench score of 8882.0 and uncached read memory bandwidth of 54.27 GB/sec, fall within the average range. Small-model LLM text generation also performs in the top tier at 367.9 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8a.xlarge offers a dense memory allocation of 8 GB per vCPU using high-speed DDR5 memory, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and a modest baseline network speed of 2 Gbps represent clear tradeoffs for storage-heavy or high-throughput networking tasks. This instance is well-suited for workloads such as memory-intensive database operations, caching layers, text processing, and single-threaded data manipulation tasks.
description: The Amazon Web Services r8a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. While the instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, it delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by a Geekbench single-core score of 2822.0 and exceptional single-threaded compression speeds. Multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth benchmarks remain in the average tier. With a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, the r8a.xlarge is designed for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and text-processing workloads that benefit from high memory capacity per core and strong single-threaded execution without requiring local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, ideal for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and exceptional single-core performance for memory-intensive data processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 32.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no local storage
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, caching, and text processing
